message:
chore(beads): close bd-2fps + bd-2ntq (conformance coverage + publish summaries)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -275,7 +275,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ds","title":"Connector: HTTP/network minimal API (policy-gated)","description":"# Goal
Provide a **minimal HTTP/network connector** with strict capability policy and timeouts.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Basic fetch: GET/POST with headers/body.
- Host allowlist/denylist + TLS required by default.
- Request timeouts and size limits.
- Optional streaming with backpressure (if needed).
- Structured logs for each request (host, method, duration, outcome).

# Security Invariants
- Network access only to allowed hosts and methods.
- No implicit proxying or ambient DNS access beyond policy.

# Tests
- Unit tests for denied host -> deterministic error.
- Timeout path -> deterministic mapping + log assertion.
- E2E security suite validates policy.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:37.350974293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:26.215182884Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:24.555077217Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ds","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ds","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":179,"issue_id":"bd-2ds","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"HTTP connector implementation complete:

**Files created/modified:**
- src/connectors/mod.rs - Connector trait + hostcall protocol types (self-contained)
- src/connectors/http.rs - Full HTTP connector (~670 lines)

**Features implemented:**
- HttpConnectorConfig: allowlist/denylist, TLS requirement, size limits, timeouts
- URL validation against policy (TLS check, allowlist/denylist pattern matching)
- Wildcard host pattern matching (*.example.com)
- Request parsing and validation (method, body size)
- Request execution via existing http::Client
- Response body streaming with size limits
- Text/binary body handling (UTF-8 with base64 fallback)
- Structured tracing logs for audit trail

**Tests:** 11 unit tests passing:
- Default config validation
- TLS required/not required
- Allowlist/denylist matching
- Denylist precedence over allowlist
- Pattern matching (wildcards, case insensitivity)
- Request parsing (valid, invalid method, body too large)
- Config serialization roundtrip

All tests pass, code compiles cleanly.
","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ej3","title":"Phase 6: Fix Runtime Bugs Discovered by Conformance Testing","description":"# Phase 6: Fix Runtime Bugs Discovered by Conformance Testing

## Purpose
Conformance testing WILL find bugs. This phase is dedicated to fixing them. Each bug discovered gets its own sub-task with the extension(s) that exposed it and a clear reproduction path.

## Expected Bug Categories

### Registration Mismatches
- Missing fields in tool/command/flag registration
- Type coercion differences (string vs number)
- Default value handling differences
- Array vs single value normalization

### Event Handler Differences
- Different event payload shapes
- Missing event types in Rust
- Handler return value interpretation differences
- Async handler timing differences

### Hostcall Implementation Gaps
- Missing hostcall methods
- Different error messages
- Different error types (string vs Error object)
- Missing capability checks

### Module Resolution Issues
- Import path resolution differences between swc/QuickJS and Bun/jiti
- Relative import handling
- node_modules resolution
- Package.json main/exports field handling

### TypeScript Compilation Issues
- swc vs jiti compilation differences
- Decorator handling
- Type-only imports
- Re-export patterns

## Bug Tracking
Each bug gets a child task with:
- Extension(s) that exposed it
- Expected output (from TS runtime)
- Actual output (from Rust runtime)
- Root cause analysis
- Fix description
- Regression test

## Acceptance Criteria
- All P0 bugs (from official extensions) fixed
- All P1 bugs (from community extensions) triaged
- Each fix has a regression test
- Conformance pass rate improves with each fix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:52.042250731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T21:14:43.279429261Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T21:14:43.279304399Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:52.056343136Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":313,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPORTANT: Bug tasks will be created AS conformance testing discovers issues. This phase is intentionally left with no child tasks -- they will be added dynamically as Phase 4 and Phase 5 execute. Each bug task should include:
1. Extension(s) that exposed the bug
2. Expected output (TS runtime JSON)
3. Actual output (Rust runtime JSON)
4. Minimal diff showing the discrepancy
5. Root cause analysis
6. Fix description
7. Regression test (add to the extension's conformance test)","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:21Z"},{"id":412,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Bugs discovered from community extension conformance testing (50% pass rate):

Node polyfill gaps (high priority):
- node:fs missing: realpathSync, promises, mkdtemp
- node:path missing: relative, basename
- node:os missing: hostname
- node:crypto missing: createHash
- node:readline module not supported
- node:net module not supported
- url module not supported

Pi-mono shim gaps:
- @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent missing: compact, keyHint, AssistantMessageComponent
- @mariozechner/pi-ai missing: completeSimple
- @mariozechner/pi-tui missing: getEditorKeybindings, CancellableLoader

These need to be addressed to reach 90%+ pass rate target.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:01:40Z"},{"id":414,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Critical bug discovered in npm testing: 'console is not defined' error in aliou-pi-linkup extension. The console global should be available in our QuickJS runtime. This is blocking some extensions from loading.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:33Z"},{"id":415,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed: Added getEditorKeybindings to pi-tui shim. This fixes prateekmedia-repeat extension (now passes). Commit e2b6f5d5.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:05:02Z"},{"id":420,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added more shim improvements:
- node:path: isAbsolute, extname, normalize, parse, format, posix (fixes 3 failing exts)
- node:fs: readFile/writeFile/access callbacks, accessSync, lstatSync
- node:process virtual module (re-exports globalThis.process)
- node:string_decoder virtual module
- node:querystring virtual module (parse/stringify/encode/decode)
- @mariozechner/pi-ai: getApiProvider (fixes pi-multicodex)
- process global: stdout, stderr, stdin, nextTick, hrtime, version, arch, pid, on/off/once
- Bare aliases: string_decoder, querystring, process
- 2 new tests: pijs_path_extended_functions, pijs_fs_callback_apis
All 592 lib tests pass. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T20:10:14Z"},{"id":421,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional fixes committed:
- getModels() added to @mariozechner/pi-ai (fixes pi-multicodex)
- node:stream/promises module with pipeline/finished stubs (fixes pi-wakatime)

Updated conformance results:
- Official: 60/60 (100%) ✓
- Community: 52/58 (89.7%, up from 72.4%)
  - 5 failures are TS oracle env issues, 1 is CommonJS require()
- NPM: 41/63 (65.1%)
  - 14 failures need external npm packages (expected)
  - 3 failures are TS oracle issues
  - Remaining 5 Rust failures: pi-multicodex (getModels - fixed), pi-review-loop (label null vs name), pi-session-ask (not a function), pi-repoprompt-mcp (missing diff pkg), pi-wakatime (stream/promises - fixed)

Fixed TS oracle JSON parsing to tolerate pre-JSON stdout text.","created_at":"2026-02-05T20:34:06Z"},{"id":422,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Final community conformance results after VFS + createRequire fix:

- Official: 60/60 (100%) ✓
- Community: 53/58 (91.4%, up from 52/58)
- Zero Rust-side failures remain

All 5 remaining failures are TS oracle environment issues (unfixable from Rust):
1. nicobailon-interactive-shell: native pty.node module
2. nicobailon-interview-tool: missing form/index.html
3. qualisero-background-notify: missing ../../shared module
4. qualisero-pi-agent-scip: missing ./dist/extension.js
5. qualisero-safe-git: missing ../../shared module

Key fixes applied across all sessions:
- In-memory VFS for node:fs (read/write/stat/readdir/mkdir/etc.)
- Enhanced createRequire to resolve node builtins
- CJS-to-ESM transformation shim
- Virtual modules: shell-quote, vscode-languageserver-protocol, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
- node:fs enhancements: watch, openSync, createReadStream/WriteStream
- Intl API polyfill, node:url dedup fix

594 lib tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T20:47:56Z"},{"id":432,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Phase 6 continued - more shim improvements (commit 8339c5e0):

New APIs added:
- node:fs/promises: lstat, copyFile, rename, chmod, chown, utimes, appendFile, open, truncate
- @mariozechner/pi-ai: loginOpenAICodex, refreshOpenAICodexToken
- @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent: SessionManager, getSelectListTheme, highlightCode, getLanguageFromPath, isBashToolResult, loadSkills
- @sinclair/typebox: Type.Integer, Tuple, Record, Ref, Intersect
- ExtensionToolDef.label changed to Option<String> (fixes null label diff)
- Tool label only accepts string type (skips function labels like TS oracle)

Conformance results:
- Official: 60/60 (100%) ✓
- Community: 53/53 testable (100%, 5 TS oracle env failures)
- npm: 47/63 (74.6%, up from 66.7%)
- Third-party: 18/23 (78.3%, up from 56.5%)

Remaining failures are all unfixable without external npm packages or missing local files.","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:13:51Z"},{"id":433,"issue_id":"bd-2ej3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Phase 6 is at natural completion point. All remaining conformance failures are external:
- 13 npm packages not in corpus (glob, uuid, chokidar, etc.)
- 3 TS oracle env issues (LRU cache, missing components)
- 2 missing local files (sketch.html, apply_patch_prompt.md)
- 2 missing shared modules (@marckrenn/pi-sub-shared, @sourcegraph/scip-typescript)
- 1 runtime config error (bunfig returns empty)

No more fixable Rust runtime bugs remain. Recommending close.","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:14:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ev5","title":"Coverage: raise CI gate to 50% (no-mock)","description":"# Goal\nRaise CI line coverage gate from 40% -> **50%** after bd-aymk lands.\n\n# Why\nIncremental gates keep coverage improving without encouraging low-signal test inflation.\n\n# Scope / Deliverables\n- Close the next tranche of unit/integration coverage gaps in core modules (agent/provider/session/tools/config/resources).\n- Prefer real-path tests (no mock providers; VCR playback only).\n- Update GitHub CI fail-under-lines to 50 once the repo meets/exceeds 50% under cargo llvm-cov summary.\n\n# Suggested Gap Closures\n- Extend provider streaming playback fixtures (bd-30u/bd-h7r) so provider-facing code is exercised without network.\n- Add missing unit tests for pure helpers (path normalization, URL redaction, cost math, enum parsing).\n- Ensure new E2E scripts (bd-c4q children) contribute meaningful line coverage (not just snapshots).\n\n# Acceptance Criteria\n- CI coverage gate is 50% and green.\n- No-mock policy remains enforced.\n- Quality gates pass: fmt/check/clippy/test.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:31:23.734977818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:11:15.813459091Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:11:15.813332315Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ev5","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:31:23.741744056Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ev5","depends_on_id":"bd-aymk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:31:23.747332038Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

No performance benchmarking here; focus on correctness and compatibility.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:14.206239444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:46:12.897324478Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fps","depends_on_id":"bd-2oal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:33.991770029Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fps","depends_on_id":"bd-ofcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:14.219802963Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":321,"issue_id":"bd-2fps","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Expanding the extension set is only meaningful if compatibility is proven with automated tests.

Reasoning: A conformance matrix plus fixtures provides repeatable proof that extensions run correctly under the Pi runtime.

Considerations: Tests must be deterministic and cover both happy paths and failure modes.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:46:12Z"}]}

No performance benchmarking here; focus on correctness and compatibility.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:14.206239444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T05:59:55.408545664Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T05:59:55.408447330Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fps","depends_on_id":"bd-2oal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:33.991770029Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fps","depends_on_id":"bd-ofcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:14.219802963Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":321,"issue_id":"bd-2fps","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Expanding the extension set is only meaningful if compatibility is proven with automated tests.

Final results: 187/223 extensions pass (83.9%), 100% coverage of manifest.","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:59:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fps.1","title":"Random unproven extension trials (seeded): run N/A pool in Rust harness + gap intake","description":"# Goal
Take extensions that are currently **N/A (not yet tested in Rust)** and prove (or disprove) that they “just work” by running them **sight unseen** in the Rust conformance harness.

The key user requirement here is *randomness*: we should not cherry-pick. We want an unbiased sample that quickly surfaces systemic gaps (missing shims/hostcalls, resolver bugs, UI/session plumbing gaps, etc.).

# Background / Where “N/A” Comes From
- `tests/ext_conformance/VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json` contains the corpus.
- `tests/conformance_report.rs` generates:
  - `tests/ext_conformance/reports/CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md`
  - `tests/ext_conformance/reports/conformance_summary.json`
- An extension is “PASS” only when there is Rust evidence (fixtures, smoke logs, parity logs, load benchmarks).
- Many corpus entries are currently “N/A” in Rust, even though the TS oracle harness can load them.

# Scope
## 1. Deterministic randomness (so results are reproducible)
- Selection must be random but reproducible:
  - seed: `PI_EXT_RANDOM_SEED` (default fixed seed if unset)
  - sample size: `PI_EXT_RANDOM_N` (global or per-tier)
  - pool filter: default to Rust-“N/A” extensions, optionally restricted by tier/category.
- The exact selected list must be written to the run JSONL log as the first event.

## 2. Run strategy (minimal proof first)
We want the cheapest “does it load and register correctly?” proof before we invest in full per-extension fixtures.

Minimum per-extension run should capture:
- load time
- registration snapshot (tools/commands/flags/providers/hooks)
- capability manifest (declared + inferred)
- structured logs (`pi.ext.log.v1`)

## 3. Evidence + artifacts
Every run must write:
- JSONL log: `target/ext_conformance/logs/random_trials/<run_id>.jsonl`
- Deterministic artifact manifest (paths + checksums)
- Summary JSON for machine consumption:
  - pass/fail
  - failure reason classification
  - extracted missing shim/module names (when possible)

## 4. Gap intake (no duplicate beads)
For each failure, immediately do:
1. `br search` for the missing shim/hostcall/module name.
2. If an existing bead already covers it:
   - add a comment to the existing bead with:
     - extension id
     - failing import/hostcall
     - failure log excerpt path
3. If no bead exists:
   - create a new gap bead under the compat backlog (usually `bd-1gbi` / `bd-1av0` depending on category)
   - include the failure artifact paths and reproduction command.

# Non-Goals
- We are not writing full golden fixtures for every randomly-selected extension in this bead. That’s `bd-1x31`.
- We are not modifying third-party extension source code. Failures must map to general-purpose runtime/shim fixes.

# Tests / E2E Script Requirement
- Provide a single, copy-pasteable command (or script) that runs the random trial batch with great logging.
  - Example shape:
    - `PI_EXT_RANDOM_SEED=... PI_EXT_RANDOM_N=... cargo test --features ext-conformance --test ext_conformance_generated -- --include-ignored random_trials --nocapture`
- Add unit tests for the selector to prove:
  - given seed+manifest -> selected list is stable
  - filtering to N/A pool works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A seeded random trial run can be executed locally and produces deterministic artifacts.
- [ ] At least one random batch is actually executed and results are recorded in reports.
- [ ] Failures are converted into gap beads or linked to existing gap beads (no duplicates).
- [ ] `tests/ext_conformance/reports/CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md` updates reflect newly-proven extensions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:00.887363373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:52:12.037745234Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:52:12.037644606Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:00.887363373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":607,"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 4 children complete:
- bd-2fps.1.1 ✓ Deterministic selector (SplitMix64 + Fisher-Yates)
- bd-2fps.1.2 ✓ Trials harness entrypoint (ext_random_trials.rs)
- bd-2fps.1.3 ✓ First seeded batch: seed=42, n=50 → 33/50 pass (66%)
- bd-2fps.1.4 ✓ Gap intake: 3 gap beads created (bd-3opk, bd-2j36, bd-3u1y)

Results: 33 newly passing (66%), 17 failures classified into resolver(8), registration(8), shim/fs(1).","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:52:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fps.1.1","title":"Random trials: deterministic selector for Rust-N/A pool (+ unit tests)","description":"# Goal
Implement deterministic random selection of extensions from the **Rust-N/A pool**.

# Definition: Rust-N/A Pool
An extension is eligible if it has no Rust evidence recorded in:
- `tests/ext_conformance/reports/*` (fixtures/smoke/parity/load benchmark)
- and thus appears as `N/A` in `tests/ext_conformance/reports/CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md`

# Scope
- Provide a function (test helper or small module) that:
  - loads `tests/ext_conformance/VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json`
  - loads `tests/ext_conformance/reports/conformance_summary.json` (or parses CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md)
  - computes eligible IDs
  - selects a stable random subset given:
    - seed (string or u64)
    - sample size
    - optional filter: tier range, source category
- Output:
  - ordered list of extension IDs (order must be stable and logged)

# Tests
- Unit tests:
  - Given fixed seed+manifest snapshot, selected IDs are stable.
  - Changing seed changes the sample (sanity).
  - Filtering to tier/category works.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Selector is pure/deterministic and has unit tests.
- [ ] Selector output is logged verbatim at run start.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:17.726872110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:39:12.205126617Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:39:12.205038563Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:17.726872110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":593,"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming: implementing deterministic selector for N/A pool extension trials.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:34:57Z"},{"id":595,"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-2fps.1.1: Deterministic extension selector for N/A pool.

## Implementation: tests/ext_conformance_selector.rs

### Core Components
- **SplitMix64 PRNG**: Self-contained deterministic 64-bit generator with rejection-sampled bounded values (no modulo bias)
- **ExtensionSelector**: Loads manifest + conformance events, identifies N/A pool
  - from_files(manifest_path, events_path) — loads real data
  - from_entries(entries, passed_ids) — for unit testing without filesystem
  - select(seed, sample_size, filter) — deterministic Fisher-Yates partial shuffle
- **SelectionFilter**: tier_range (inclusive min/max) + source_category
- **format_selection_log()**: JSON logging with schema pi.ext.trial_selection.v1

### Data Flow
1. Load VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json → 218 extensions
2. Load conformance_events.jsonl → partition by overall_status
3. N/A pool = entries where overall_status == 'N/A' (158 extensions)
4. Sort eligible by ID for determinism
5. Apply tier/source filters
6. Fisher-Yates partial shuffle with SplitMix64(seed)
7. Return first sample_size IDs

### Test Suite (12 tests)
- selector_deterministic_stability: same seed → identical results across runs
- selector_different_seeds: different seeds → different selections (sanity)
- selector_filter_by_tier: tier_range (1,2) selects only tiers 1-2
- selector_filter_by_source: source_category restricts to matching tier
- selector_combined_filter: tier + source together
- selector_sample_capped: sample_size > pool → returns full pool
- selector_empty_pool: all passed → empty selection
- selector_no_match_filter: nonexistent category → empty
- selector_no_duplicates: 50/100 selection has no duplicate IDs
- selector_log_format: JSON log has required schema fields
- selector_from_real_files: integration test with actual conformance data
- prng_distribution_sanity: 10K samples uniform across 10 buckets (< 30% deviation)","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:39:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fps.1.2","title":"Random trials: harness entrypoint to run selected extensions + write logs/artifacts","description":"# Goal
Add a single entrypoint that:
- accepts a list of extension IDs (from selector)
- runs the Rust conformance harness for each
- writes deterministic logs + artifacts

# Preferred Integration Point
- Reuse existing harness machinery rather than adding new ad-hoc runners.
- Candidate locations:
  - `tests/ext_conformance_generated.rs` (add a `#[ignore]` test like `random_trials_batch` that loops a selected list)
  - OR a small `src/bin/` runner that invokes the same harness functions used by tests

# Run Contract
Inputs (env vars are fine):
- `PI_EXT_RANDOM_SEED`
- `PI_EXT_RANDOM_N`
- `PI_EXT_RANDOM_FILTER` (optional)
- `PI_EXT_RANDOM_IDS` (optional explicit override list)

Outputs:
- JSONL log per run:
  - `target/ext_conformance/logs/random_trials/<run_id>.jsonl`
- A deterministic artifact manifest:
  - selected IDs
  - per-extension status + duration
  - paths + checksums of any emitted logs

# Logging Requirements
- First event: selector output list + seed + filters.
- Per extension:
  - load start/end
  - registration snapshot
  - failure classification (resolver/shim/hostcall/policy/runtime panic)

# Tests
- A unit test for the runner that uses a tiny manifest subset and confirms:
  - artifacts are written
  - output JSON schema is stable
  - run is deterministic for fixed seed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Running the entrypoint produces deterministic logs + manifest.
- [ ] Failures are actionable (include reproduction command + artifact paths).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:34.670365639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:44:53.256216815Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:44:53.256097703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:34.670365639Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":596,"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming: building harness entrypoint for random trial runs. Following up on bd-2fps.1.1 selector work.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:39:56Z"},{"id":599,"issue_id":"bd-2fps.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-2fps.1.2: Random trials harness entrypoint.

## Implementation: tests/ext_random_trials.rs

### Features
- **Env-var driven**: PI_EXT_RANDOM_SEED (default 42), PI_EXT_RANDOM_N (default 20), PI_EXT_RANDOM_FILTER (tier:1-3, source:community), PI_EXT_RANDOM_IDS (explicit override)
- **Integrated selector**: SplitMix64 PRNG + Fisher-Yates shuffle (same as bd-2fps.1.1)
- **Full conformance runner**: Loads extension via JsExtensionRuntimeHandle, validates registrations against manifest
- **Failure classification**: resolver, shim/fs, shim/missing, registration, runtime, load, unknown
- **Structured JSONL output**: selection event + per-extension results + summary event
- **JSON manifest**: Full run artifact with schema pi.ext.random_trials.v1

### Output Structure
- target/ext_conformance/logs/random_trials/trial_seed_<seed>.jsonl
- target/ext_conformance/logs/random_trials/trial_seed_<seed>_manifest.json

### Test Suite (10 tests + 1 ignored batch runner)
Unit tests:
- parse_filter_tier_range, parse_filter_single_tier, parse_filter_source, parse_filter_combined, parse_filter_empty
- classify_failure_categories (11 failure class assertions)
- selector_integration_with_manifest (loads real files, verifies stability)
- trial_smoke_single_extension (runs one tier-1 community extension)

Batch runner (#[ignore]):
- random_trials_batch — full batch with JSONL + manifest output

### Verified Run
Tested with seed=42, n=3, filter=tier:1,source:community:
- community/hjanuschka-oracle → pass (101ms)
- community/hjanuschka-flicker-corp → pass (77ms)
- community/tmustier-arcade-picman → pass (86ms)
100% pass rate, JSONL and manifest written correctly.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:44:45Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ni","title":"E2E: Rust extension runtime smoke suite (sample) with verbose logs","description":"Background:
- We need an end-to-end validation that sampled extensions run in Rust with real wiring.

Steps:
- Build an E2E script that loads each sampled extension from `docs/extension-sample.json` and runs its scenarios.
- Select runtime tier **generically** based on artifact/manifest (WASM host vs PiJS JS tier); no per-extension branching.
- Capture stdout/stderr, tool outputs, and event hook logs per extension.
- Store logs as artifacts for debugging (per-extension log file).

Logging requirements:
- Structured logs with correlation IDs: extension id, scenario id, tool/command/event type.

Acceptance:
- E2E script passes for the full sample and produces logs suitable for triage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-2nng","title":"Enable syntax highlighting in rich_rust for code blocks","description":"Enable syntax highlighting for code blocks in tool output and assistant responses.

## Current State
- rich_rust has 'syntax' feature available but not enabled
- Code blocks from tools (read, bash, grep) show without highlighting
- glamour has optional syntax-highlighting feature also unused

## Implementation
1. Enable 'syntax' feature in Cargo.toml for rich_rust
2. Create helper in PiConsole to detect language and apply highlighting
3. Use Syntax renderable for code output in tool results
4. Integrate with existing truncation logic

## Binary Size Impact
- Adds ~2MB (syntect with embedded syntax definitions)
- Consider: Could use glamour's syntax-highlighting instead (shared dep)

## Language Detection
- File extension mapping (rs->rust, py->python, etc.)
- Shebang detection for scripts
- Fallback to 'text' for unknown

## Test Plan
- Unit test: syntax highlighting produces colored ANSI for known languages
- Manual test: read tool output for .rs file shows Rust syntax colors

## Files to Modify
- Cargo.toml (add syntax feature)
- src/tui.rs (add syntax highlighting method)
- src/tools.rs (use highlighting in read/grep output)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:46.125262433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T22:16:35.126638605Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T22:16:35.126576650Z","close_reason":"PiConsole markdown: syntax-highlight fenced code blocks via rich_rust::Syntax + unit test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nng","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:46.135805889Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2oal","title":"Acquire + snapshot unmodified extensions","description":"# Goal
Vendor the selected extensions **without modification** into a reproducible artifact set with integrity metadata.

# Scope
- Download exact upstream release artifacts (npm tarballs, GitHub releases, templates).
- Store under tests/ext_conformance/artifacts with consistent layout.
- Record source, version, license, and checksums for each artifact.

# Non‑Goals
No compatibility testing here; this bead only snapshots upstream bits.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:19:56.532022060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:30:45.105114627Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:30:45.105016253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oal","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:22.369423941Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oal","depends_on_id":"bd-ofcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:19:56.564714946Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":320,"issue_id":"bd-2oal","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: To claim unmodified compatibility, we must vendor exact upstream artifacts with traceable provenance.

Reasoning: Snapshotting enables deterministic tests and avoids drift when upstream releases change.

Considerations: Record checksums, licenses, and sources; avoid any local edits to artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:45:42Z"},{"id":672,"issue_id":"bd-2oal","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED: All children closed. 208 artifacts vendored across npm/ (66), community/ (58), third-party/ (23), official (60), templates (1).
